Ellyn Baratheon
Ellyn Baratheon, by yaerenart ©
Allegiance House Baratheon
Culture Stormlander
Born 113–129 AC[1]
Father Borros Baratheon
Mother Elenda Caron
Books
  • Fire & Blood (mentioned)
  • The Rise of the Dragon (mentioned)
Played by Laura Lake (uncredited)
TV series House of the Dragon: Season 1

Ellyn Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys I, Aegon II and Aegon III Targaryen. She was one of the four daughters of Lord Borros Baratheon of Storm's End with his wife, Lady Elenda Caron.[2][3] Ellyn and her sisters, Cassandra, Maris, and Floris, were known as the Four Storms.[4]

In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Ellyn is portrayed by Laura Lake.

Appearance and Character[ ]

See also: Images of Ellyn Baratheon

Ellyn was less pretty than her sister Floris, and less clever than her sister Maris. She and her sisters would sometimes fight, but their father always made them make peace afterwards.[2]

History[ ]

At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Aemond Targaryen arrived at Storm's End. Lord Borros offered the hand of any of his daughters. Prince Aemond agreed to a betrothal, though the identity of the daughter is not known, save it was not Maris. The lord and prince were haggling over dates and dowries the morning Prince Lucerys arrived, who claimed he could not marry Ellyn or any of her sisters due to his betrothal to Rhaena Targaryen, and was sent away by Lord Borros.[2]

Lady Elenda sent Cassandra, Ellyn, and Floris to represent House Baratheon at the coronation of Aegon III Targaryen and his wedding to Princess Jaehaera. Ellyn remained at court as a companion of Queen Jaehaera Targaryen while her mother departed the capital.[4]

After Queen Jaehaera died, Lady Elenda put forward Ellyn and Cassandra as potential brides for Aegon III. She attended the Maiden's Day Ball and Ellyn asked the king if he liked her gown. Her sister later put it about that her question was if he liked her breasts.[3]
